mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications requiring efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and potential complexity in managing distributed deployments.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Milvus CLI to interact with the Milvus database.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/releases/download/v2.5.0/milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv milvus-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create milvus-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Update kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Milvus
steps.description:
Deploy Milvus on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.5.0 --namespace milvus --create-namespace

```

> Add the Milvus Helm repository.
> Install Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for Milvus.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring resources.
> Deploy autoscaling resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
Remove Milvus and all associated resources. This will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstall the Milvus Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Backup existing data, upgrade the Milvus installation, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl cp milvus-pod:/path/to/data /backup/location

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.6.0 --namespace milvus

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Backup data from the Milvus pod.
> Upgrade to the new version of Milvus.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Milvus.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release for Milvus may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Milvus using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to migrate your data to the new indexing format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus service running with the output of 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
